description: A clean, professionally designed White House infographic / bar chart on a white background. The headline at top, in a dark navy serif font with the words 'Bring Down' italicized, reads 'The First President to Bring Down Prescription Drug Prices.' A bold subhead reads 'Change in prescription drug CPI, first 16 months of presidential term,' with a smaller note 'Note: CEA analysis of BLS data.' The chart shows fifteen navy vertical bars labeled by president/term across the x-axis (Nixon, Nixon, Carter, Reagan, Reagan, Bush 41, Clinton, Clinton, Bush 43, Bush 43, Obama, Obama, Trump 45, Biden) with a y-axis from -3% to 15%; the final bar, labeled 'Trump 47,' is colored red and is the only bar below zero (about -2%), visually emphasizing that Trump's second term uniquely reduced drug-price CPI while all prior terms saw increases. At bottom, a navy footer bar carries a small White House building emblem and 'The WHITE HOUSE' wordmark. The technique is a comparative statistics card using color contrast (lone red negative bar vs. a sea of navy positives) to dramatize a favorable claim. Target audience: general public / media, to tout an economic-policy win.
notable_text: The First President to Bring Down Prescription Drug Prices; Change in prescription drug CPI, first 16 months of presidential term; CEA analysis of BLS data; The WHITE HOUSE
